attempts.strategy: iter-152 alg-closed three-step collapse: (1) X integral (irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton + isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced) so Gamma(X) is a field via isField_of_universallyClosed; (2) properness gives appTop finite hence integral over k via finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed transferred across GammaSpecIso by RingHom.finite_respectsIso.2; (3) IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral gives algebraMap bijective hence range = top, surjectivity of f.appTop.hom recovered via Function.Surjective.of_comp
attempts.code_tried: set f := X ↓ Spec (CommRingCat.of k); haveI : IrreducibleSpace X := GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton f; haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X; letI : Field Γ(X, ⊤) := (isField_of_universallyClosed k f).toField; let F := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ f.appTop; have hFinF : F.hom.Finite := by apply RingHom.finite_respectsIso.2 (e := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv); exact finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k f; ...; have hbij := IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral; rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]; ...; exact hFsurj.of_comp
attempts.insight: RingHom.finite_respectsIso.2 precomposes finiteness by a ring iso — exact analogue of the isIntegral_respectsIso.2 step in the Mathlib isField_of_universallyClosed proof. F.hom = f.appTop.hom.comp (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom holds by rfl. Subsingleton (Spec (.of k)) is an available instance so irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton needs no manual haveI. ~25-30 LOC; axiom-clean (propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound; no sorryAx, verified).

Differential.ContainConstants / mem_range_of_deriv_eq_zero is for an ABSTRACT single derivation B->B (Differential B instance), not the universal Kahler derivation K -> Omega[K/k]; only shipped instance is trivial ContainConstants A A. Algebra.FormallyUnramified.iff_isSeparable + Subsingleton Omega[L/K] only cover the ALGEBRAIC (EssFiniteType) case where Omega=0; the function field K=Frac B has positive transcendence degree so does not apply. Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Field gives FormallySmooth for separably-generated extensions but exposes NO basis of Omega[K/k] from a separating transcendence basis and NO kernel description.
